text: Taoism diagnoses distraction as a product of wei — forced, willful action — rather than of insufficient effort. The person who cannot focus is not failing to try hard enough; they are trying in the wrong register entirely. This inversion challenges the dominant Western model of attention as a resource to be hoarded and deployed through willpower. Psychologist Roy Baumeister's ego-depletion framework, which held that self-control draws from a finite reserve, has faced significant replication challenges — suggesting that the effort-based model of attention may itself be culturally contingent rather than neurologically inevitable.
question: What challenge to the effort-based model of attention does the passage describe?

options.text: Neuroscientists have identified unlimited attentional reserves in long-term meditators
Eastern traditions consider effort irrelevant to attentional training
Roy Baumeister's ego-depletion framework has faced significant replication challenges
Digital distraction has made willpower-based models obsolete in modern settings
